stem: A urban planning study measures the commute time (minutes) for a sample of 40 city workers. The sample mean is 161.6 min with a sample standard deviation of 28.9 min. Assuming the data are approximately normally distributed, estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Population mean ≈ 161.6 min. 95% CI = [152.3, 170.8]. Point estimate: 161.6 min.

common_failure_modes: Using z-critical value instead of t-critical for small samples
Not reporting the confidence interval alongside the point estimate
